title: Incorrect macro expansion inside string literals
body:
This code:

```verilog
`define M(foo) "foo foo foo"

module top;
  initial begin
    $display(`M(bar));
    $display(`M({1, 2 + b}));
    $finish;
  end
endmodule
```

will print
```
bar bar bar
{1, 2 + b} {1, 2 + b} {1, 2 + b}
```

The expected output is
```
foo foo foo
foo foo foo
```

One of the big 3 makes the same mistake.

Section 22.5.1 of the latest standard explicitly says "Macro substitution and argument substitution shall not occur within string literals", then proceeds to provide an example which Verilator also produces the wrong result for.
